mnemonics: PIM-POM: Primitives In, Pass Object Memory (reference)
Java is Pass-By-VA: Value for primitives, Address (reference) for objects
keyPoints: Primitive arguments are passed by value; a copy of the data is made.
Object arguments are passed by value-of-the-reference; a copy of the memory address is passed.
Changes made to an object's state inside a method are permanent because the copied reference still points to the same object.
commonMistakes: Believing that a method can change the value of a primitive variable passed to it.
Believing that reassigning an object reference inside a method will change the original reference outside the method.
Confusing 'pass-by-reference' with Java's 'pass-by-value' for object references.
